subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **Companion app** (web-based, required to play — provides narration, music, and story branching)
- **Ship board** (tracks hull, supplies, crew, guns, and sails)
- **Map board** (ocean exploration)
- **Player character sheets** (unique story books for each pirate)
- **Location cards** and **event cards**
- **Treasure tokens** and **supply tokens**
- **Ship standee** and **location standees**
- **Action tokens** (wooden discs per player)
- **Skill tokens** (used for skill checks)
- **Pirate meeples** (1 per player)
- **Dice** (for skill checks)

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Open the **companion app** on a tablet or computer and select a scenario.
2. Place the **ship board** in the center — set hull, supplies, crew, guns, and sails to their starting values as directed by the app.
3. Place the **map board** nearby — the app will tell you where to place starting location tokens.
4. Follow the app's **scenario-specific setup** instructions, which vary by adventure.
5. Place supply tokens, treasure tokens, and dice within reach.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Each player selects a **pirate character** and takes the corresponding character sheet/story book.
2. Fill in your pirate's **name** and mark starting stats as directed by the app.
3. Each player takes a set of **action tokens** in their color.
4. The app assigns a **first mate** (first player) role.
5. Read the opening scenario narration from the app — this sets the stage for your adventure.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
Forgotten Waters is a **cooperative narrative adventure** where 3-7 players are pirates on a shared ship, living out a branching story. The companion app narrates the tale, presents choices, and tracks story progression.

Players must work together to keep the ship seaworthy while also advancing their **personal pirate stories**. Each character has a story book with entries that unfold based on their choices — you need to complete enough of your story to achieve your personal ending.

The game is **scenario-based** — each scenario is a self-contained adventure lasting 2-4 hours.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each round represents a period of time in the adventure:

### 1. Narrative Phase
The app presents a **story scene** with narration (often voice-acted). The crew discusses the situation and the app presents choices.

### 2. Planning Phase
The current **location** shows available action spaces (displayed on a location page referenced by the app). Each action space has a limited number of slots.

Players discuss and then **simultaneously place their action tokens** on available spaces. Actions include things like:
- **Explore**: Discover new locations or story elements.
- **Forage**: Gain supplies.
- **Repair**: Fix the ship's hull.
- **Train**: Improve your pirate skills.
- **Story**: Advance your personal pirate story.
- **Special actions**: Location-specific narrative options.

### 3. Action Phase
Resolve each action space in order (numbered). The app guides resolution:
- Some actions require **skill checks** — roll dice and add your relevant skill bonus. Compare to a target number.
- Some actions trigger **story entries** — read the corresponding entry in your story book or the app narrates.
- Some actions affect **ship stats** (hull, supplies, crew, etc.).

### 4. Ship Phase
- The ship **consumes supplies** (crew eats!).
- Check for **events** — the app may trigger storms, encounters, or story beats.
- If any ship stat hits zero, bad things happen. If the hull reaches zero, the ship sinks and everyone loses.

### 5. Navigation Phase
The crew decides where to sail next on the map (if applicable). Move the ship standee and continue the adventure.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
### Skill Checks
When a skill check is required:
1. Roll the specified number of dice.
2. Add your relevant **skill bonus** (from your character sheet).
3. Compare to the **target number**. Meet or exceed = success.
4. The app or card tells you the result of success or failure.

### Ship Management
- **Hull**: Ship health. If it reaches 0, the game is lost.
- **Supplies**: Food and resources. If it reaches 0, crew morale plummets and bad events trigger.
- **Crew**: Your pirate crew (NPCs). Losing crew reduces your effectiveness.
- **Guns**: Ship combat strength for naval encounters.
- **Sails**: Ship speed, affecting navigation options.

### Personal Stories
Each pirate has a **story book** with numbered entries. By taking story actions and making choices, you unlock entries that advance your personal narrative. At game end, you check if you've completed enough story entries to achieve your pirate's **happy ending**.

### Constellation Track
Each player has a **constellation track** that fills in over the game. Reaching certain thresholds unlocks abilities and story progress.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
### Cooperative but Personal
The game is cooperative — if the ship sinks or a scenario objective fails, everyone loses. However, each player also has a **personal win condition** (completing their story). It's possible for the group to survive but individual pirates to fail their personal stories.

### Timed Decisions
Some app-driven decisions have **timers** — the crew must decide quickly or the app chooses a default (usually bad) option.

### Replayability
Scenarios have **branching paths** — replaying the same scenario with different choices leads to very different stories.

### Read-Aloud
The app features **voice-acted narration**. It's designed to be theatrical and immersive — lean into the pirate theme!

### No Character Death (Usually)
Pirates don't die during the game. Instead, failure results in setbacks, injuries, or ship damage.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
The scenario ends when the **story reaches its conclusion** (determined by the app).

### Group Win/Loss
- If the ship survived and the main scenario objective was completed, the **group succeeds**.
- If the ship sank or the objective was failed, **everyone loses**.

### Individual Success
- Each player checks their **constellation track** and **story progress**.
- If you've completed enough of your personal story, you achieve your pirate's happy ending.
- It's possible to have a group win but personal failure (or vice versa).

There is no point scoring — it is a narrative experience with pass/fail outcomes.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Communicate with your crew**. Discuss action placement openly — you need to balance ship maintenance with personal story advancement.
- **Don't neglect the ship**. It's tempting to focus on your personal story, but if the hull or supplies hit zero, everyone loses.
- **Advance your story steadily**. Don't leave all your personal story progress to the end — you may run out of time.
- **Listen to the narration**. The app often hides hints and clues in the story text.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Balance ship duties**: Rotate who handles repairs, foraging, and other ship maintenance. No one player should sacrifice their entire game for the ship.
- **Skill specialization**: Each pirate has different skill strengths. Play to your character's strengths when choosing actions requiring skill checks.
- **Story timing**: Some story actions are more time-sensitive than others. Read your story book entries to understand what you need.
- **Supplies buffer**: Always try to keep supplies at least 2-3 above zero. Running out triggers cascading problems.
- **Embrace the narrative**: This game is about the experience. Make choices that create interesting stories, not just optimal outcomes.
